name: pi_default_disc
note: Layout (CHIPBleServiceData.h:53-136): byte0 OpCode = 0x00 (struct is always zero-initialized via Init() for the standard DeviceIdentificationInfo block -- confirmed via GenericConfigurationManagerImpl.ipp:604-634, OpCode is never explicitly set elsewhere). kchipBLEServiceDataType_DeviceIdentificationInfo = 0x01 (CHIPBleServiceData.h:42) is a separate legacy enum for a different purpose (BLE GAP AD structure service-data type), NOT this struct's OpCode byte -- do not confuse the two. byte1 = discriminator low 8 bits (0xF00 & 0xff = 0x00). byte2 = (advVersion<<4) | discriminator high 4 bits (SetDeviceDiscriminator/SetAdvertisementVersion, CHIPBleServiceData.h:80-108) = (0<<4)|0xF = 0x0f. bytes3-4 = VendorId LE (SetVendorId, LittleEndian::Put16) = 0xFFF1 -> f1 ff. bytes5-6 = ProductId LE = 0x8000 -> 00 80. byte7 = AdditionalDataFlag bit0 (kAdditionalDataFlagMask=0x1, C3/has-additional-data) | bit1 (kExtendedAnnouncementFlagMask=0x2, extended) = 0x00. Pure layout encode from the given (discriminator, VID, PID) inputs, not observed from any device -- no live capture needed to validate this arithmetic.

name: mixed_fields
note: Same layout as pi_default_disc. byte0 OpCode = 0x00. discriminator 0xABC: byte1 (low8) = 0xbc, byte2 low nibble (high4) = 0xa, adv_version 0 -> byte2 = (0<<4)|0xa = 0x0a. VendorId 0x1234 LE -> 34 12. ProductId 0x5678 LE -> 78 56. byte7 = 0x01 (kAdditionalDataFlagMask set, extended announcement clear). Pure layout encode, cross-checked byte-for-byte with an independent Python re-implementation of the struct's bit-packing.
